title: VARIETY Reviews 20
wikitext:
[]: VARIETY Reviews 2.0             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Nov 16, 2009 3:22 pm Nice little notice in the world's most vital entertainment news center. Reviewer doesn't really know what he's talking about (acceptable, though I rather love EOE) and the review is surprisingly positive. [url]http://www.variety.com/review/VE1117941597.html?categoryid=31&cs=1[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Mon Nov 16, 2009 3:58 pm Can it still be considered a review if they're mostly interested in predicting how much money it will make in the States?

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Nov 16, 2009 5:30 pm Hah, Richard said fan reaction to EoE was generally unfavorable.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Mon Nov 16, 2009 6:38 pm NAveryW wrote: Hah, Richard said fan reaction to EoE was generally unfavorable.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Tue Nov 17, 2009 3:32 am Electric Sachiel wrote: Generally i thought fans were quite divided over whether they liked or didn't like EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Tue Nov 17, 2009 12:20 pm anime about teenagers defending the world from machines of mass destruction

[]:             Quote Post by Cailet » Tue Nov 17, 2009 9:25 pm Axell wrote: anime about teenagers defending the world from machines of mass destruction

[]:             Quote Post by jayngfet » Tue Nov 17, 2009 10:04 pm Cailet wrote: Axell wrote: anime about teenagers defending the world from machines of mass destruction

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Wed Nov 18, 2009 1:49 am the reviewer's facts are a tad sketchy I have to say but IIRC, this is probably the most mainstream account of something related to Evangelion that has gotten this much publicity in the U.S. I wonder now if there is "a stirring" in Hollywood for some future project involving Evangelion with arguably the most respected publication among the Hollywood establishment taking any anime from Japan other than GitS or a Miyazaki film seriously, much less Evangelion of all things

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Wed Nov 18, 2009 2:00 am Cailet wrote: Technically yeah but would you want to try explaining the Angels in a way that made sense and didn't spoil the fuck out of what actually happens?

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Nov 18, 2009 3:56 am Ah- No and NO. They're one of the world's largest entertainment publications and they just happen to cover everything that has a theatrical release. They even wrote a review for the Ass to Mouth Horror Film The Human Centipede. A three paragraph review in the back alleys of Variety means as much as the review for 1.0 did- jackshit. It's cute. Nothing more.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Nov 18, 2009 9:42 am Gendo'sPapa wrote: A three paragraph review in the back alleys of Variety means as much as the review for 1.0 did- jackshit.

[]:             Quote Post by Cailet » Wed Nov 18, 2009 10:52 pm SaltyJoe wrote: Cailet wrote: Technically yeah but would you want to try explaining the Angels in a way that made sense and didn't spoil the fuck out of what actually happens?

[]:             Quote Post by Taekmkm » Thu Nov 19, 2009 12:09 am But in the first 8 minutes, the damn thing is pulsating with gills and an extra head. It's about as mechanical as a slim jim.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Thu Nov 19, 2009 11:34 am That was excellent review, actually

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Thu Nov 19, 2009 12:42 pm Dunno why they said fan reactions to eoe were mostly negative, though not everyone likes it most eva fans I've seen tend to like eoe.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Fri Nov 20, 2009 1:03 am Gendo'sPapa wrote: They're one of the world's largest entertainment publications and they just happen to cover everything that has a theatrical release. They even wrote a review for the Ass to Mouth Horror Film The Human Centipede.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Nov 20, 2009 5:21 am Variety does a lot of reviews that are not Ghibli/GitS actually: http://www.variety.com/review/VE1117931891.html?categoryid=31&cs=1 http://www.variety.com/review/VE1117927864.html?categoryid=31&cs=1 http://www.variety.com/review/VE1117908291.html?categoryid=31&cs=1 http://www.variety.com/review/VE1117903529.html?categoryid=31&cs=1 http://www.variety.com/awardcentral_review/VE1117931477.html?nav=reviews07&categoryid=2352&cs=1 http://www.variety.com/review/VE1117941214.html?categoryid=31&cs=1 http://www.variety.com/review/VE1117940835.html?cs=1 http://www.variety.com/review/VE1117916603.html?categoryid=31&cs=1

[]:             Quote Post by Pcnerdjack14 » Fri Nov 20, 2009 5:53 pm The review was okay I guess, wish it went into detail a bit more.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Fri Nov 20, 2009 11:23 pm Ornette wrote: Variety does a lot of reviews that are not Ghibli/GitS actually
summary:
[]: VARIETY Reviews 2.0             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Mon Nov 16, 2009 3:22 pm Nice little notice in the world's most vital entertainment news center. Reviewer doesn't really know what he's talking about (acceptable, though I rather love EOE) and the review is surprisingly positive. [url]http://www.variety.com/review/VE1117941597.html?categoryid=31&cs=1[/url]
